title: The top five AI trends reshaping banking in 2026
excerpt: As AI adoption accelerates, the focus shifts from implementing AI tools to embedding AI safely and effectively across core banking operations. Tamsin Crossla...
content_html:
GlobalData

Tue, July 21, 2026 at 10:04 AM EDT 5 min read

Artificial intelligence is rapidly moving beyond experimentation in banking. What began as isolated pilots inside innovation labs is now being embedded directly into core operational workflows across payments, compliance, fraud operations and software engineering.
The conversation has fundamentally shifted from "Should we use AI?" to "Can we operationalise AI faster and more safely than competitors?" Here are the areas where some of the most meaningful transformation is already happening:

1. Moving beyond chatbots

One of the fastest-growing AI implementations across tier 1 banks is the rollout of internal employee copilots spanning engineering, operations, risk, compliance and customer servicing teams.

Unlike the early generation of standalone chatbots, these generative AI assistants are increasingly integrated directly into enterprise knowledge bases, workflow systems and business applications to retrieve information, summarise content and support employee decision-making, significantly reducing the time spent searching for information and completing routine tasks.

Banks are now moving beyond AI assistants into the next phase of AI implementation: agentic AI. Rather than simply generating responses, AI agents can plan and execute multi-step operational workflows, interact with enterprise systems, update records, initiate business processes and escalate decisions where appropriate. In customer servicing, this enables end-to-end handling of requests, reducing friction while improving consistency, response times and personalisation.

This marks an important transition from AI assistants that primarily support employees and customers with information and recommendations to operationally integrated AI agents capable of executing end-to-end servicing processes. The result is reduced servicing friction, faster response times and increasingly personalised customer experiences.

2. Accelerating software engineering transformation

With institutions under pressure to modernise legacy infrastructure while controlling costs and addressing engineering shortages, AI-assisted development is seeing rapid adoption across the industry. Coding copilots, automated testing, code translation and documentation generation are helping banks improve developer productivity and reduce delivery timelines across large-scale transformation programmes.

Given the scale of legacy technology estates still operating across financial services, many institutions now view AI-assisted engineering as strategically critical to accelerating broader transformation programmes.

content_html_zh:
环球数据

2026 年 7 月 21 日星期二上午 10:04（美国东部时间） 阅读 5 分钟

人工智能正在迅速超越银行业的实验范围。 最初是创新实验室内的孤立试点，现在已直接嵌入到支付、合规、欺诈操作和软件工程等领域的核心运营工作流程中。对话已经从根本上从“我们应该使用人工智能吗？”转变。 “我们能否比竞争对手更快、更安全地运用人工智能？” 以下是一些最有意义的转变已经发生的领域：

1. 超越聊天机器人

一级银行增长最快的人工智能实施之一是跨工程、运营、风险、合规和客户服务团队的内部员工副驾驶的推出。

与早期的独立聊天机器人不同，这些生成式人工智能助手越来越多地直接集成到企业知识库、工作流系统和业务应用程序中，以检索信息、总结内容并支持员工决策，从而显着减少搜索信息和完成日常任务所花费的时间。

银行现在正在超越人工智能助手，进入人工智能实施的下一阶段：代理人工智能。 人工智能代理不是简单地生成响应，而是可以规划和执行多步骤操作工作流程、与企业系统交互、更新记录、启动业务流程并在适当的情况下升级决策。 在客户服务中，这可以实现请求的端到端处理，减少摩擦，同时提高一致性、响应时间和个性化。

这标志着从主要为员工和客户提供信息和建议的人工智能助手，到能够执行端到端服务流程的操作集成人工智能代理的重要转变。 其结果是减少了服务摩擦、加快了响应时间以及日益个性化的客户体验。

2. 加速软件工程转型

由于各机构面临着对遗留基础设施进行现代化改造、同时控制成本和解决工程短缺的压力，人工智能辅助开发正在整个行业得到快速采用。 编码副驾驶、自动化测试、代码翻译和文档生成正在帮助银行提高开发人员的生产力并缩短大规模转型计划的交付时间。

鉴于仍在金融服务领域运营的传统技术产业的规模，许多机构现在将人工智能辅助工程视为加速更广泛转型计划的战略关键。
